title: Finding Eigenvalues using NumPy
description: Program to compute eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a matrix
code:
import numpy as np

A = np.array([[3, 1], [1, 3]])
eigenvalues, eigenvectors = np.linalg.eig(A)

print("Eigenvalues:", eigenvalues)
print("Eigenvectors:", eigenvectors)

# Verify Av = λv for first eigenvalue
eigenvalue = eigenvalues[0]
eigenvector = eigenvectors[:, 0]
result = np.dot(A, eigenvector)
expected = eigenvalue * eigenvector
print("Verification (Av = λv):", np.allclose(result, expected))
explanation: Uses NumPy's linear algebra library to compute eigenvalues and eigenvectors. The verification step confirms Av = λv holds true.

title: Characteristic Polynomial Calculation
description: Algorithm to compute characteristic polynomial coefficients
code:
CHARACTERISTIC_POLYNOMIAL(A)
BEGIN
 n ← rows(A)
 I ← IDENTITY_MATRIX(n)
 // Compute determinant of (A - λI) symbolically
 // For 2×2: |a-λ, b; c, d-λ| = λ² - (a+d)λ + (ad-bc)
 trace ← 0
 FOR i ← 1 TO n DO
 trace ← trace + A[i][i]
 END FOR
 det ← DETERMINANT(A)
 coeff1 ← -trace
 coeff0 ← det
 RETURN polynomial: λ² + coeff1*λ + coeff0
END
explanation: Builds characteristic polynomial using trace and determinant. For 2×2 matrices, polynomial is λ² - tr(A)λ + det(A) = 0.

title: Finding Eigenvectors by Gauss-Jordan
description: Algorithm to find eigenvector for a given eigenvalue
code:
FIND_EIGENVECTOR(A, λ)
BEGIN
 n ← rows(A)
 M ← A - λ * IDENTITY_MATRIX(n)
 // Solve M * v = 0 using Gauss-Jordan elimination
 reduced ← GAUSS_JORDAN(M)
 // Extract null space basis vectors
 // For 2×2: If reduced = [[1, a], [0, 0]]
 // Then eigenvector satisfies x + ay = 0
 // Let y = 1, then x = -a
 // Eigenvector v = [-a, 1]
 RETURN eigenvector
END

DIAGONALIZE(A)
BEGIN
 eigenvalues ← FIND_EIGENVALUES(A)
 eigenvectors ← []
 FOR EACH λ IN eigenvalues DO
 v ← FIND_EIGENVECTOR(A, λ)
 APPEND(v, eigenvectors)
 END FOR
 P ← MATRIX_FROM_COLUMNS(eigenvectors)
 D ← DIAGONAL_MATRIX(eigenvalues)
 RETURN P, D
END
explanation: Finds eigenvectors by solving homogeneous system (A - λI)v = 0. Diagonalization collects eigenvectors as columns of P.
